Fundamental: true hidden for the bonus requires the bonus in the batch, which requires knowing it pre-verify. Fold is at its structural optimum

notes: VERDICT for the fix: (A) fused multi-row verify FA (grid.z=row, per-row t_kv/n_splits derived inside via the same fa_split_keys formula) attacks the 21% + T x combine/launch; (C-class) partial-accept replay ELIMINATION (per-column recur-state rebuild from verify's own bit-identical columns + KV truncate + pending-bonus-on-partial) attacks the bigger 44% by cutting ~0.54 duplicate trunk reads/round. Design B (key-major shared walk) DEAD on arrival: per-row split boundaries derive from t_kv_r (per=ceil(t_kv_r/n_splits_r)) so rows do NOT share fa_decode's partition except at 64-boundary coincidences — cannot be partition-exact for general r. Draft-side 17% is acceptance/model-bound (lm_head), not engine cost.

change: MULTI-ROW FUSED VERIFY FA (design A): fa_decode_vec_q_rows + fa_decode_combine_rows — ONE launch runs fa_decode_vec_q's exact per-row program for all T verify rows (grid.z=row, per-row t_kv_r=base+r+1 and n_splits_r=ceil(t_kv_r/split_keys) derived IN-KERNEL from the same fa_split_keys formula; splits>=n_splits_r exit unwritten, row-combine merges its own n_splits_r in the same ascending order). Replaces T x (fa_decode+combine+2 dtod copies+5 partial allocs) in full_attn_verify. Engages when base_len+1 >= FA_VEC_MIN_TKV (all rows vec-path, mirrors eager kernel pick); BW24_FA_ROWS_OFF=1 = per-row-loop seam.

label: positive
notes: p2 gains more than p3 because at 6k the draft chain (q6_K lm_head 1.06ms x ~2.7 drafts/round) and the partial-accept replay (duplicate trunk pass) own a growing share. NEXT (profiled): kill the partial-accept replay — verify columns are already bit-identical to eager, so accepted KV rows can stand, recur state can be rebuilt from stored gdn inputs with the SAME gdn_scan kernel, and the bonus can ride pending into the next verify like the full-accept path.

change: REPLAY-FREE PARTIAL ACCEPT: the verify's first j=base+n_acc columns ARE the committed sequence (bit-identical to eager per the decode-exact contract), so partial rounds now KEEP them — KV truncates to pos+j, recurrent state rebuilds from a per-round VerifyCkpt (batched linear layers: prefix re-run of the SAME gdn_scan kernel (t=j) from the snapshot state over the retained token-major inputs — the kernel's t-loop carries state in registers, iteration t independent of T; conv ring: pure-copy ssm_conv_ring_rebuild_f32 from raw stored inputs; per-column-path layers: dtod state clones after each column) — and the bonus stays PENDING like the full-accept path. The legacy duplicate trunk replay (~0.54 extra full weight reads/round) is GONE. BW24_SPEC_REPLAY=1 legacy seam; j==0 rounds (pre-first-pending only) keep legacy rollback+replay.
kernel: ssm_conv_ring_rebuild_f32 (pure copy) + gdn_scan_s128 prefix re-run + decode_step_t_core/VerifyCkpt

label: positive
notes: The exactness argument: (1) emitted tokens unchanged — bonus rides pending into the next verify exactly like the proven full-accept fold; (2) kept KV rows == what a replay would recompute (verify-probe contract); (3) gdn prefix re-run = same kernel, same inputs, fewer loop iterations => state after j tokens bit-identical; (4) ring rebuild + column clones are pure copies. Enables retiring ~44%-of-wall duplicate MMVQ measured in the spec-longctx-profile record.

change: (3a) TRUE-HIDDEN REFRESH: every round overwrites ALL committed positions' draft-scratch entries via one batched mtp_kv_fill from the verify's exact hidden stack (already resident: the ckpt keeps vx) — replaces chain-approximate entries; the reference engine's mtp_update design. BW24_SPEC_NOREFRESH=1 seam. (3b) HEAD-LESS PSEUDO-SEED GRAPH: second capture of the MTP forward without lm_head/argmax/prob for the once-per-round pseudo replay (h_nextn's inputs untouched -> identical seed; tok_d/p_d are round-start-reset anyway) — retires the draft head's full weight read (~1.06ms q6_K on 9B) per round.

Per-column accumulators are independent, so cross-column reordering is exactness-FREE; only per-accumulator order is pinned.

Do not build without new information of a different KIND (e.g. the verify becoming launch-bound again via much faster kernels, or T growing past MMVQ's batched range). The REAL 27B levers, ranked by this trace: (1) qmatvec_nvfp4_mmvq_b4 efficiency — 63% of the whole ROUND is this one kernel's execution (prior clamp attempt negative; needs ncu source counters); (2) acceptance 0.58->0.75 (draft quality — llama same head reaches ~0.75 at n-max 3); (3) 9B: b4+b2 MMVQ 41% + fa_rows 16% of verify. 9B verify kernel mix: nvfp4_b4 23.6%, fa_rows 16.3%, nvfp4_b2 13.0%, q45k/q6k b4+b2 ~19%, quantize_q8_1 3.5% (252 launches!).
